title: Does It Matter?
content:

title: Glory Of Women
content:

content:

content:

title: The Dug-Out
content:

title: The Road
content:
The road is thronged with women; soldiers pass
    And halt, but never see them; yet they're here -

title: Base Details
content:
If I were fierce, and bald, and short of breath,
     I'd live with scarlet Majors at the Base,
    And speed glum heroes up the line to death.

title: Dreamers
content:
Soldiers are citizens of death's grey land,
     Drawing no dividend from time's to-morrows.
    In the great hour of destiny they stand,

title: Suicide In The Trenches
content:

title: Banishment
content:
I am banished from the patient men who fight.
    They smote my heart to pity, built my pride.
    Shoulder to aching shoulder, side by side,

title: They
content:

content:

content:

title: Remorse

title: Lamentations
content:

    And blundered in. With puzzled, patient face

content:

content:

content:

content:

content:

title: Invocation
content:

title: Thrushes
content:

title: To Victory
content:

    Not in the woeful crimson of men slain,
    But shining as a garden; come with the streaming

title: Joy-Bells
content:

title: Wirers
content:

title: The One-Legged Man
Break Of Day
content:

    In a dank, musty dug-out where he lay,

title: Blighters
content:
The house is crammed: tier beyond tier they grin
    And cackle at the Show, while prancing ranks
    Of harlots shrill the chorus, drunk with d

title: In An Underground Dressing-Station
content:
Quietly they set their burden down: he tried
    To grin; moaned; moved his head from side to side.

title: A Letter Home. (To Robert Graves)
content:
I

    Here I'm sitting in the gloom
    Of my quiet attic room.
    France goes rolling all around,
    Fledged with forest May has crowned.
